title: Rotorua Geothermal Parks — Te Puia vs Wai-O-Tapu Guide
meta_desc: Rotorua geothermal parks guide for families, compare Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate
intro: Rotorua is home to some of New Zealand's most unique geothermal parks, but which one is best for your family? With prices ranging from $30 to $100 per person, it's essential to choose wisely. In this guide, we'll compare Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate to help you make an informed decision.

sections.heading: Overview of Rotorua Geothermal Parks
sections.body: Rotorua is a 3-hour drive from Auckland and a 1-hour drive from Taupo. The city is home to three main geothermal parks: Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate. Each park offers a unique experience, with a range of geothermal features, cultural performances, and outdoor activities.

rows: Geysers, mud pools, kiwi bird sanctuary
Lady Knox Geyser, champagne pool, artist's palette
Mud foot spa, geothermal walk, sulphur spa

sections.heading: Best for Families
sections.body: Te Puia is a great option for families, with a kiwi bird sanctuary and a range of interactive exhibits. Wai-O-Tapu is also a good choice, with a ladybug-themed playground and a range of easy walking trails. Hell's Gate is more suited to older children and adults, with a focus on relaxation and rejuvenation.

sections.heading: Getting There and Around
list: Public transport: Bus services available from Rotorua city centre
Shuttle services: Available from Te Puia and Wai-O-Tapu

sections.heading: Tips and Essentials
sections.body: Be sure to wear comfortable walking shoes and bring sunscreen, a hat, and a camera. Each park has its own cafe and restaurant, but you can also bring your own picnic lunch. Don't miss the cultural performances at Te Puia, which include traditional Maori dances and carvings.

cta.text: Calculate your trip costs with our NZ family travel budget calculator

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body: Rotorua has a range of accommodation options, from budget-friendly motels to luxury hotels. Consider staying in the city centre for easy access to the geothermal parks and other attractions. You can also rent a campervan and stay at one of the nearby campsites.
